This is a this is morning So we'll just I guess we'll just jump right in Has anybody done direct mail here in the room? Oh Good everybody and everybody's had big successes with it Yeah, usually people say direct mail sucks, you know, I've tried it I wasted a bunch of money, you know Okay, so we've got we've got all over the map some people at work some people it doesn't so the first thing that we're gonna talk about is the actual process for getting people to the webinars and There's a couple of different routes that you can take and one of the things that I like to do and this people don't like this but Tough there isn't one way to do it There isn't a hey this works and that's all there is there's a lot of different ways to do it And it depends on your market It depends on your offer and it depends on you know your price point all that kind of stuff But we're gonna go through two basic ways of using postcards and and building out the funnel So the first one is going to be where you have the postcard See if that's big enough. No, that's a little postcard. That's just the stamp Okay, and on the postcard this first method we're gonna show is going to be a URL So obviously the stamp would be on the other side and we're gonna We're I'll walk you through exactly what the postcard looks like but we're gonna use a URL style first and The postcard is going to take them right to a landing page and The landing page you can do a couple of different things The registration page the registration pages that Russell just went over Are fantastic, but I think that when you're using direct mail one of the advantages that you have is you already have all of their data Or most of it sometimes you might not have an email address or sometimes you might not have a phone number But a lot of times you have all of their data So there are things that you can do there's the there's the sophisticated way and then then there's the kind of down and dirty way The down and dirty way would be just postcard right to the landing page And it would go right to one of those landing pages like you saw earlier and just walk them through the process Just as if they came from Google or any other paid traffic source What's what we've been doing recently is actually putting all of that Information, you know when you rent the list into a database so that when they come to a landing page like this We're not using pearls. Who's familiar with what pearls are? We're not using pearls. I haven't had great luck with pearls some people have a Pearl is a personalized URL so what it would be is is You would have a postcard with the person's name and so you'd have like let's say that your this is your your web address and Then it would you know have their name here And so it looks like you built a website for them or sometimes they're done as a sub domain in front, you know And the idea is you've got this personalized website built for them that they come to and they can see and When they were new they got a lot better traction than they do now and and you know some people still have really good success with them I'm not I'm not for them or against them. They haven't worked for me very well. I find that At least recently what's working for us is if we use a postcard and something Something on the postcard. I'll bring that back here You don't have to use these exact words, but the words that I like to use on the postcard is We've been Trying to Reach you and we use this either sometimes as the as the pre-head or sometimes as the actual headline on the postcard and when you do that and you're using a Database like we're talking about here. It's congruent because what happens is if I go back to this They get the postcard they come to the landing page and what we would do in front of that landing page like Mike's Both of the mics were using is on the first page. They're still getting the the email verified back the some people who especially when I talked to people who Most of their life has been spent doing marketing online Think that there's a there's a big disconnect here and think well You know, I just want everything to be online I just want them to if you're going to drive the traffic from offline to online I just want them to come online and and that way I don't have to mess with all this extra infrastructure the number of leads and the number of responses you'll get will be Tremendously higher if all you do is make somebody pick up a phone rather than go to a website in Most cases that's my disclaimer. Yes It's a great question Every time I've ever tested that it has gotten the lowest response You don't want to give them a decision to make you want to make you just commit to one or the other and split test You know use Segments of the same list and and do one with the phone and one with the URL and test it because your market might be one that You know if they go to a landing page at the same rate that they go to a phone number That's great because it's less cost. You just go. Oh, are we still mailing those lists? I mean Here's the different different The in value wise this is going to be the number one Most of the time this is going to be number two and Most of time this is going to be number three in terms of like what they're actually worth to you So let's define what they are obviously buyers is pretty easy But compiled lists are what most people think of when they think of direct mail and it's usually where most businesses Start because they think that That's really kind of all they they have compiled lists are things like Demographic information like you could get a list of everybody that has blue eyes in your state from the air you know from the state driver's license department or Everybody that's got a license to practice, you know certain type of medicine or everybody that you know you get the idea Compiled lists are groups of people that all have a trait or couple of traits similar The reason they're less likely to be a Good fit for what we want to do is they haven't demonstrated anything other than the fact that you know They have brown hair or they have you know a certain income or you know, whatever That kind of information the demographic information is great to lay on top of the other list so we'll get to that part but compiled lists let's just have somebody who has a Weird category like besides Jennifer. I don't know Jennifer's here Okay Okay, so your initial reaction when somebody that's in your boat And I've actually worked with people that do that same thing is well Here's here's the typical profile of my client of my ideal client. They're this age They have this many kids and they you know Living this kind of neighborhood you could narrow it down to zip codes and things like that The thing is you can make lists like that work, but they're really difficult What I'd rather have and it might not work for you But what I'd rather have is people who've already worked with somebody else that's got a it's like looking for a JV This is the beautiful thing about you know list research is it's like looking for a JV partner, but you don't have to give them half You just get to rent their list There you go, so now I would go on to SRDS or Next mark comm next mark is my favorite place to go look to go research lists Everybody know who bent a next mark You your eyes will pop out when you go there and spend any amount of time just studying When you went through the course Russell is that one of the first things you did is go to next mark Yeah, it is like a Clearing it well, it's not like it's it's a clearing house for all the available commercial lists that are on the market And so when you it's kind of like if you were looking for JV partner It'd be kind of like going down the list of like you know your your What did what did check call it the power 100 the dream 100 be like looking down your dream 100 list except for Almost all of them except for the really small players almost all of them rent their mailing lists If I'm in the health category, and I want everybody that has a GNC gold card I can rent that list if I want everybody that joined Bally's health club last month I can rent that list like you don't have to go ask for permission They just it's on the open market. You can rent it So whatever your category is next mark is a great place to go start doing that research It's like a search engine for mailing lists. You can type in Very detailed information or very vague information and start to narrow it down The Okay, so where we're okay, so That's that those are responders list. I mean compiled list so responders lists That's going to be the next one up on the scale a Responders list is somebody that has responded to something but not necessarily by Bought it so they could have responded to a magazine ad another direct mail ad An online internet offer we rent several lists of people that are what in the CPA network They call them partials people that have filled out part of the information, but didn't give their credit card information We rent lists like that on a regular basis, and they do very very well for us in a couple of categories And so those those who would fall into the responders category You're probably wondering like so, you know, how am I going to know if they're responders or compiler their buyers? Well when you go to next marker you go to your list broker There's what's called a data card and then on the data card it gives you all of this information about every single one of them a Lot of the people that that write the data cards lie and exaggerate and And basically lie So you have to be very careful about Lists that are not known, you know like if it's not something where you know like a valet or a GNC or some Company that you've heard of before you need to be very careful and ask the list broker More details about it because a lot of times on the list on the on the data card They'll say things like you know this person bought you know Everybody on this list spent at least a hundred and twenty nine dollars on something to make their health better Well, that's a little vague I need to get some more information about them and so your list broker will usually Be able to give you some more information about them not always though and sometimes the people who put the lists up for rent Don't want to give you that information because they don't want to be copied, you know they're they're trying to protect themselves and So if you've got a list that looks attractive to you But you can't get enough information about it the next question to ask the list broker is about continuation continuation simply means the number of other people that have come and Rented this list before and then bought and then rented it again because if they've continued on it then obviously It's working for people and you can usually see a list The broker will give you lists of other companies that have continued on it And if it's one of your competitors, you know, hey, they're they've gone on with it's probably gonna work for me Buyers lists are Your holy grail. They're the best that really is like doing a JV with somebody How who in here would love it if you could just go rent Russell's list and just you know, he would let you just mail it be awesome, right? In your category in lots of categories, there are many people put their lists up for rent Many in fact most of the big ones because they understand that that rental revenue is is a really nice source of income When I when usually when I talk to internet people who are just really Buried in the internet marketing world and they find out that you can literally just go rent somebody's list They feel like wow, yeah, it's almost like Yeah, I mean just picture that Russell would you put your list out there right now like on an Excel spreadsheet for everybody to just go take Yeah, I don't think so and yet people will put them up for rent and when you go to rent them to mail Typical price in that category hundred and ten bucks a thousand Not 50% So people When you know that you can actually go target your competitors lists Especially if you're a small fry and your competitors are huge their lists a lot of times are gigantic So some things to look out for when you're going to rent lists The data cards usually have a lot of lies on them one area that they that they Fudge a lot is the dollar amount sold So typically let me draw out what a data card actually looks like a little bit. Is that who has seen a data card before? Okay, quite a few of you every Site does it a little bit different or brokerage it but typically they're going to have a description here They're going to have the Number that's put up like the universe And Also the hotline When you when you're looking for lists when I'm looking for lists, I'm usually looking for a list As big as I can find but usually small enough that Okay, if a list is gigantic it's probably either really weak Or the person that's putting the list up is lying about the source of it because there's just not Gigantic lists out there you'll go on there and you'll get really excited because you'll see in my category I just found this one list where there's two million names on it and they're adding 500,000 new names a month a Test it it's worth a test, but it's probably not going to happen usually you're looking for a list that has a Hotline somewhere in the range. I mean Every category is a little different but what I'm looking for is a list that has a hotline somewhere in the range of about 10,000 new names a month and A universe that goes back at least you know two years or so Why why why do I care if it goes back to yours because if it's brand new? Even if it works really good, they might not be around and so I can't really bank on it I want something that's got a little bit of stability Yes, yeah compiled lists is a complete I There's not a whole lot to learn about compiled lists. They are what they are you can see it It's very easy test them if they work for your category. That's awesome The holy grail is if you can get the white pages to work You know not too many people have an offer that can make that work and so what you're trying to do is you're trying to balance between specifically Narrowing it down and keeping it as big as possible to make it as profitable as possible So you don't have to worry about that really with compiled lists really I'm talking about buyers lists In the in the categories that I like to do a lot of business in there will be Any given time there may be there might be 20 lists like this Out of Hundreds and hundreds that are available so don't get hung up on the fact that there might only be a couple of lists I mean if they're if they're big enough that you can and don't look at this as your only traffic source obviously But it's a really great steady one to kind of like you know Smooth out the the ups and downs and ups and downs of some of the other online stuff Okay My missing anything about buyers lists kind of does suck without my slides here Yes Yes Okay For certain categories I have got list brokers that I could recommend to everybody For other categories you're gonna have to do your own research because I don't I don't know list brokers in every single category List brokers tend to specialize I'll give you my little real estate speech and those who have gone through my course, you know, I've heard this a million times List brokers the easiest way to think about list brokers is they're like a real estate agent and so That you know lots of realtors will specialize in you know luxury homes or foreclosed homes or HUD homes or whatever This brokers are the same way They get to a point where they have several clients in one category and they get really good at knowing that category And you want a broker like that because your list broker is sort of like To use an online analogy be like having a rep at Google telling you what other people are doing You want a broker that can give you that kind of information and a general broker You know somebody that you like you just found and they said oh, yeah, we can get any list Yeah, they're probably not gonna help you a lot You're gonna sort of be on your own they can get the list for you But they're not gonna give you any kind of recommendation or any kind of insight information I've had the same list broker now for about 12 years and the nice thing about that relationship is Over time he's gotten comfortable with like letting out information that man I'd be pissed if he was letting out my information like that, but I'm okay when he's given it to me from somebody else, you know that Hey, did you hear what so-and-so is doing is is the kind of numbers he's doing really what what list is he mailing? Oh, well, he started mailing this you want a broker that's gonna you know work with you and they'll do that not First of all, it's just human nature. They like to gossip Second of all if you're a long-term customer They want you to keep coming back and if they can give you good information You're gonna keep coming back and renting more and renting more and keeping it going so Here's another way that that whole industry works like a like the real estate industry and it's probably the easiest one for people to actually Relate it to the if you're if you own a list and you want to Put it up for rent you go to one of these agencies and so that's kind of like you listing your house the listing agent Gets a piece and then the broker who actually finds the customer to rent the list gets a piece And so what they're gonna try to do most of the time Especially if you're new is they're going to try to get you they're gonna if you the list broker that you go to you start Talking about what you want to mail and what category you're in and they're gonna try To make recommendations for house lists lists that they own the listings because they get to double dip on those So it's not that's not a bad thing It's not any more money out of your pocket But it does sometimes make them recommend lists that they wouldn't recommend otherwise Because they they're getting a double commission instead of a single commission So, you know imagine you're the listing agent for a house you want to sell that house You don't want to just list that you want to sell it and you know that way you get both commissions If you're a first time You know somebody coming to rent lists sometimes unscrupulous brokers, you know, that's what they're gonna do They're gonna just try to rent you the biggest file that they possibly can and something in-house because they're gonna get all the commission So what if you're savvy and you know that and you know those questions and you bring that up? They're gonna be put on the spot and they won't do it normally One of the things that a lot of people come up against when they start into a direct mail campaign is And they go to start renting lists the first time as they come back to me They say Luke this guy's saying I have to rent 10,000 names at a time to start a lot of them will ask for that But that's just the starting point. I've never rented 10,000 on a first run with a list ever And if the if the list good if the list is really good Sometimes they'll just give you a thousand if they if they think you're a player Because they want you to go rent it. They want you to go mail it and come back and buy the whole file You're you're I'm getting right there that's perfect perfect question though every Categories different and you'll get people that will tell you I have a very different opinion from like some of the guys That do direct mail who will say well you need to test 50,000 to know whether you have anything That's gonna work I said to me that's ridiculous if I can't make it work on a couple of thousand And it's probably not gonna work by by testing more if you are in a category that has lots of available lists It's different than if you're in a category where there's you know one or two lists Nitin and I were talking last night about personal therapists or Physical therapists. I'm sorry personal therapists. Of course, they're all personal They're not group therapists In that category he's going after a group that there is sort of a finite group of them That you know, there's there's new ones coming in but it's not this ever-expanding group in a in a market like that He doesn't have to test much at all. I mean a thousand two thousand and you're gonna have a pretty good idea of What's gonna happen with the rest of it? In the business opportunity categories, which I do a lot of mailing in I will have lists that have worked for Everything I've ever mailed and then I'll have lists that only work on certain things So if you're if you're working with your broker and you tell them hey listen, I'm doing XYZ online with my my lead gen and direct mail something. I really would love to do I'm not gonna give you know a bunch of money to this I really want to just test this out as cheap as I can if you had to make it work, mr. Broker What list would you use? And it sounds obvious, but I mean That's what that's what I would say and they're gonna come back and they're gonna give you a recommendation They're gonna say yeah, and the owner wants five thousand five thousand minimum and then you'll say well great But I don't really want to rent more than two thousand Can you take that back to them and literally it is just like the real estate process? They're dealing with the list owner and so they'll come back to the list owner and the list owner will be like You know screw him. I don't want to you know if he's not willing to rent five thousand I don't want to do it There are cases where I would still go ahead and rent those anyway Just don't mail them all You know the the list rental is like the second cost Postage is the biggest cost so if you're gonna if you're going to waste money like if the list is horrible Why not find out for a couple of thousand even if you had to rent, you know five thousand? right I Want to lay out I want to spend some time laying out the postcard because that's a big part of doing this But the list thing is really huge so I want to make sure that I'm not like glossing over anything Is there any other questions about lists? Yes Yeah, it's completely different because we're using it for something else that would be great for a house file where you actually know a Lot about the list in a lot of these cases We know one or two or three bits of information about them and that's it and so no I treat it more like it's a PPC campaign They're gonna see it and they're either gonna do it or not Yeah, yeah There's no stupid questions oh That's a really good question you cannot buy mailing lists most of the time there are some mailing List companies that every once in a while put lists up for sale, but most of the time you're renting them for one time use So when you pay that fee you get to mail it once So using the analogy of Russell giving everybody his list If you get to mail it once you don't get to mail it you know ongoing Lee for you know 50 times this month Yeah You get from unlimited months I mean there's no there's no it doesn't run out and you can read and you can mail them again You just have to pay the rental fee again Yeah, yeah like and a lot of times real quick I'll get to and a lot of times if I find a list that I really Got a huge response out of and it's small like let's say that the whole universe of this list is 20,000 names a lot of times If I've gotten a great response out of the first three or four thousand and I intend to mail the rest I'll go back to the broker and before I've rented the rest. I'll come back and I'll say listen I think we're gonna mail this one three times Can you go negotiate a multi-use rate and on a multi-use rate? You're gonna get your price way way down and now I get to mail it three times four times whatever we agree on Okay, depends on your process you're talking about going to a webinar or you're talking about just selling in general Yeah, I mean to go to a webinar it really You want to have that price point high because you're gonna have a lot of falloff between the response and the In the actual getting through the whole funnel, which is why you need the price point higher in a you know in a webinar format Right doesn't mean you can't sell with postcards low-end stuff. That's what I sell most of most of what I sell is low-end stuff I've used it for driving to a webinar. We also use a you know completely other process We're not going to get into today for selling low-end products, and then you could do the webinar after that Yes That's awesome if you know like so they let you know what the list is Right Right And the clothes rates decent when they show up and all Okay Okay, that's it. That's another really good question But before I get to that though the the part about the mailhouse I wouldn't personally let somebody else make all those decisions But for if it's working for you keep using it, you know keep doing it The reason is when you have one of those sort of black box, you know companies like that that says hey We'll do all this for you the challenge with that is I don't know What the names are where they're coming from and if they're only doing compiled which it sounds like demographically That no matter how good you're doing with it You could be doing better by renting lists of people that bought that you know red light green light trading software like I Would go after people who've already bought stuff similar and probably had a horrible experience because it's a lot of its crap Yeah, so but they've already demonstrated the interest Yeah, that's my point they're not buyers you want to mail a buyer's list yes I have some students that are in the UK and doing great I could not specifically tell them Like I don't know how much your stamps cost. I don't know some of the regulations you have but the processes are the same oh Yeah, there's list brokers in the UK. Oh, yeah No, but I could get one yeah, absolutely absolutely yes Right okay, so that's another thing because people automatically think well They're sending me the list I rented it once but I got it right Most of the time they're not sending it to you They're gonna send it to your printer or your mail house or whoever's actually gonna do the mailing in some cases though And in some categories like in the business opportunity category a lot of those list owners don't know any better and you do get them directly I get all mine directly You've got to show the file yeah So that's gonna be different from case to case But yeah, don't be don't be surprised if you don't ever get to see the list Gosh see all of the lists that I use I get them so I never really thought about that, but you're right I Would have I would ask the list broker if there's a way to do it that they would be comfortable with because there I mean you could technically you could do it You could do it with the sequel database that they have access to and then you're just having an API read the information out of it I mean you want to get technical you could figure out a way to do it You don't run into it in a lot of categories though, but in some categories you do Yes I Depends on what you're selling it depends on what you're selling I don't know if I would I don't know if I would use that to drive somebody to a Webinar where I was gonna sell something for a high price. I Mean they're coupon clippers there people the people that are gonna respond to that are looking for ten dollars off a big man Or a buck. I don't know how much a big man cost whatever I don't eat that stuff Yeah, but I mean that's like saying you can avoid the price of An online PPC ad by going and placing it in a free classified section. Yeah, so what I mean if you don't get anything out of it It doesn't really matter if it was free No No, I don't Okay, so we ready to move on to like the layout of the postcard and that okay I Know this isn't none of this is nearly as sexy and exciting as like online stuff and social media and SEO and all that kind Of stuff, but the thing that I really want and I do do a lot of that stuff as well I mean I'm not just a direct mail person But the the part of my business that stays the steadiest is the part that's the direct mail because it's so predictable Like I said, we have lists that we've been mailing for ten years straight That don't change that don't call me up and go hey you can't mail that postcard again Well, you know they don't care as long as they get the list rental fees. They don't care, okay? Two different approaches that I've used again. I like to always state that These are not the only ways I've seen other lots of other things at work, but You know before I get to that I want to make sure that this is kind of drilled into everybody I get reminded of this all the time and because sometimes I forget And that is We don't want to have Any of these marketing processes that we're laying out do anything except for getting getting them to the next step and It's very easy to forget in fact. I've had many very well known Marketing folks not just online marketing folks, but old grizzly guys Tell me that well, yeah You just you can't use postcards to a cold market and make it work And I like I'm in the midst of a campaign that's doing fifty thousand a week I'm like, yeah, actually you can and part of the problem is Especially people that have a copywriting background is they want to fill this postcard up with copy and they want to try to tell the whole Story on a postcard. Well, you know, I've paid the postage I might as well fill it up and it's for me It's been the exact wrong approach and the reason that we've been able to do so well with postcards is we only ask the postcard to Do one thing and that's to get them to take the very first action either It's going to pick up the phone and make a 800 call or go to a URL and that's it and Somebody just showed me an example of a postcard just before we walked up here and thought you thought you had it right because we talked about this before and it already was asking for something so what the Postcard was selling was the webinar and I would submit that if you try to do that you're gonna even if you get it to work You could do better if you start before that and what I mean by that is I would not try to Sell the webinar on The postcard I would try to sell them on going to the URL or the 800 number one or the other Every single step just has it's just like you know Russell was saying where you didn't ask for the email They just asked for the date. When did you want to do it same thing here? So with that in mind with there's two styles of postcards that I've had a lot of success with One is called the official style. I've just named them this And an official style postcard The layout well the layout is gonna look the same because I don't I can't draw fonts with this marker But the layout is gonna be basically a pre-head head Three or four bullets sometimes five a Deadline I always like to put deadlines on these on the official ones and Then the call to action whether it's an 800 number or URL not both 800 or URL and To make these even more official looking I usually put down here in the bottom In little tiny like seven point Font Official form number and then I give it a little number so it looks like it came out of something official That's just a made-up number sometimes is tracking number the other side of the postcard is going to have nothing Except for the name and address that it's going to Except for in some cases if you're mailing bulk or the letter shop that you're using Won't let you use their permit without a return address then you would put a return address I try to not use a return address on the postcards as much as I possibly can there are sometimes when you have to and People out so why wouldn't we use a return address? I mean, I don't care if they know but except for one reason that I care No, that would actually be good. I could take the Nixie That's one that's one reason Well, you're gonna get some of that you're Know the biggest reason is and I found this I never believed it until we tested it side by side But people have prejudices against different parts of the country and so they'll say, you know somebody in California Or New York I wouldn't buy anything from somebody in California. It's from California. It must be a joke, you know And you think that that I'm exaggerating but it makes a huge difference. So if you can get away with it Use no return address There are cases where you do want to use it like for example if you've got something to do where you can tie in something that Make sense for that region So if you're mailing something and you and I'm not suggesting you have permission But let's say you had permission on a Nutritional thing or something like that to mail on behalf of the Mayo Clinic Well, I was sure it held put the Mayo Clinic return address on there because that's credibility, you know So there are ways that you would times when you would use it, but for the most part I try to not use that What you want to do with let me describe because this doesn't really give it a good description my the slide I had built for this you would have seen exactly what this looks like usually this card is laid out in a Courier font so it looks like It actually looks very old and it looks like it came out of like a dot matrix printer or you know something like that Because you want it to if you're going for this look the official look and I'll give you some uses for the official Look here in a second, but if you're going for this look you want it to look like it came out of some sort of Official process like the government or doctor's office or something Who's gotten direct mail that looks like that that looks official you always pay more attention to it Most of the time you pay more attention to it I've used a card like this to sell Painter leaf products that we rented lists of we were talking about this last night When Jennifer was up here and I said that you can actually rent lists of people that have certain ailments There are compiled lists available for every ailment you can possibly think of Now those lists are compiled after the person's gone to the doctor's office ostensibly or sometimes they're Compiled from the prescriptions being filled at drug stores So there are HIPAA regulations that you can't there's certain things you can't say To that person directly or they won't rent you the list like you if your postcard said you know Hey, I just found out you got you know bladder problem They won't rent you the list to mail that But you could rent the list and this is how we get around it and your list brokers got to really be willing to work with you You can say things like We've been trying to reach you or something to that effect That and then the way that you get around the statement is do you know anybody? Do you or anybody you know suffer from bladder problem like they do with the TV commercials? That you can do you can't say that I know you have it But that kind of anyway, we've used a card like this to sell pain relief products and The card we had a doctor that endorsed the product and the doctor was actually a part an equity partner in the business and so We made the card to look like it was a notice coming from your doctor People paid attention to it and it said you know this is regarding it So it had a little headline. We've been we've been trying to reach you regarding had a little re regarding Back pain and your new pain relief protocol Whoa the doctor sent me something about my new you know like The the phone rang off the hook and we had there was a Nutritional supplement. I mean a natural based pain relief product. We had 3,000 people on Continuity within about three weeks of starting that mailing. It was very very successful No No, well, I mean we'll get to that but no, that's that's the case where you're gonna have a horrible response usually Sometimes Sometimes but most of the time, you know, I would see if Bob's doing that And if he's got a really product with a really great product with a really great story, I just go knock it off. I Mean unless it's something that I really it's like you got to just dig in deep and it'd be a big process Then I would just yeah, but no normally this is I It goes beyond the scuffle we're gonna talk about here But normally I teach you go look for the market and the product and then you build this out to fit it As opposed to you know, you've got a widget and let's see how we can sell your widget this way That's why I said this doesn't work for everything and B2B is a perfect example It will never get past the gatekeeper that postcards going right in the trash B2C it works fan that fantastic Yes The bigger postcards is just a waste of your money the whole advantage of mailing a postcard There's basically only two advantages using a postcard over a letter postage is cheaper and it's got full frontal nudity John Carlton's line basically you don't have to wait for them to open it. It's there That's right in their face. So you don't have to get it open It's like an email that is already open when they get there So those are the only two advantages if you start going to the bigger postcards And I know there are people that swear by them and have had great results I guarantee I could redesign that postcard as a small one and save them postage and get the same response The big postcard is usually a waste of money Haven't yet I would only attempt that in a market that I knew was tech savvy because The vast majority of like internet marketers were all hip to that and think oh that's awesome And you go to a convention and everybody's got it on their t-shirts and your scan in the shirts The world doesn't work that way yet It will it'll get there But right now I don't think I think that you'd cut out a big part of your market if you made that your only response device And if you made it a response device in addition to the others, I think you're going to dilute your your response If I was mailing like you know subscribers to a wired magazine sure, you know depends on your market There was a question over here, okay? Okay Okay, let's get through the second type of postcard and then that's absolutely absolutely So everybody gets the idea and some people have gotten mail like this of what the what I mean by an official postcard The kinds of markets that this would work for let's go to this before we move on to the other style of postcard You know this works fantastic in the markets it works in but it doesn't work for everything I mean in some markets that I'm in I mean this would never work if you're selling anything that Has to do with if you can like on the financial stuff if you can tie in some sort of regulation That's coming down or anything that sounds like it came from the government I've worked with people that Used it to sell you know how to get a grant guide I've used it with people who sold business opportunities where they were doing HUD and FHA fund Refund processing and so it looked like it was coming from a government agency But like I said we've used it for a pain relief product because it looked like it's coming from a doctor anything where you can have the air of Efficiency this style works really really well Okay, if if you if it doesn't work for that I mean if you're if you're niche or your Category doesn't fit that then the other style of card that I use I use this one more often Is the I call this the marketing style card? for lack of a exciting way of putting it and on these cards I Will usually have a pre-head a two or three line headline and one or two paragraphs of you know little body copy and then a call to action and my call to action is usually because this is a marketing card It's gonna have like you know, this would be like a dotted line you know a little box With Call blah blah blah Couple of if you're if you're gonna use a URL then this doesn't matter, but if you're gonna use an 800 number You always want to include the words 24-hour toll-free recorded message Because it lowers the barrier when they don't think they're gonna you know call and hear a salesman and get accosted They just they can just very casually call On a lot of the the stuff that we do the 800 number is a recorded message But at the end of the recorded message we give them the option to press one to order and so a sale does happen later But they can start without having to you know Commit to any of that the marketing style card. Here's where a lot of people fall down with this. This is the the Probably for most markets the best type of card to use But they try to sell too much here All you want to do with this is just peak curiosity and get them to the either the URL or the phone number That's it. You try to do anything more than that That's where it screws up and a lot of like big-time copywriters that have told me that postcards don't work or don't work Except for the house list. That's what I when they I see their postcard I say well, yeah, well, you're trying to sell everything in the postcard There's not enough room there to sell anything other than what you're trying to get them to do So if you if you try to do that it's going to fail because what happens somebody gets that postcard and if that's the whole thing Or it's trying to do too much They're making their decision right there whether to do anything or not and so your response You don't even have a chance to do follow-up or anything else the other thing that's beautiful about Using this the database system is that if they get part way through it. We have you know email follow-up We have phone follow-up We have direct mail follow-up because there's one thing about we talked earlier about renting the list you're renting it at First the minute somebody responds to you. That's your name now you own them You don't have to sell them anything for you to be able to you know, it's it's like an opt-in No, this is not bullets I'm this is going to be paragraphs a marketing style card. I usually don't use bullets I'll use like paragraphs. It'll be like You know quick little headline The pre-head for me most of the time is going to be something very simple It's going to say free 24-hour recorded message reveals and then what and then the big reveal on the headline What how I discovered using postcards and getting them to work I came this is this is Relevant because this will help you understand how to write what goes on there My background before doing direct mail was doing classified ads I did a lot of classified ads in fact I actually worked for you know, Donald Priya the classified ad guy and built multi-million dollar businesses doing just classified ads and When I started like dabbling in direct mail I first just was using letters because I didn't think he could do anything with postcards and then One day I was at my printer's office and he had this palette of postcards and I you know, I read the postcard and it was Somebody that was obviously one of my competitors and I said does that is that working for him? He was yeah I mean we'll print another palette like that next week and I said oh Maybe I should look into postcards and so when I decided to give it a try I came from that background of like in a classified ad so think about PPC if you've ever written an ad for PPC You have to be really succinct and to the point and you've got to get them to click now right think of the postcard the same way You can't sell anything think of it as a Facebook ad or a Google ad If you only had so many characters, what would you put on there? And that's how you have to think about you have way more characters to work with but a lot of times Some of the best postcards I've written are ones that are like, you know two sentences in a called action Because again, you're not trying to sell anything except for the call or the URL It's just you're getting the click and If you can remember that part that's the part that Most people fail at as they'll go and they'll get excited and go wow I have all this space and they'll start describing their product and I'm like whoa whoa you haven't even got him to call yet Don't you let's not talk about your product? Okay, so any questions about that style okay, that's pretty straightforward, but it's you got to be short and to the point Easy enough Okay Let's see Nutritional supplements software mailing lists sales leads travel clubs $100,000 sandwich franchises. How about that home study courses consumer benefits clubs pre-po-ed Back in the old days prepaid phone cards Music clubs dental plans Consulting alarms credit card protection coaching Books reports of all different styles. Like billing So you could afford to do it then because really here's what it takes to do postcard mailing That's what we didn't even cover this Currently in the US. I don't know what it is in the UK, but currently it's about 450 bucks a thousand give or take depending on your list rental list rental is somewhere between 85 to You can go as high as three hundred dollars per thousand most of the lists that you're gonna find that are gonna be effective are gonna be in that 110 to 125 dollars per thousand category postage just went up to 29 so $290 thousand and your printing is the smallest cost because oh there's another thing. I never ever I shouldn't say never I Less than 1% of the time use a color postcard almost always It's this is plain black ink on colored card stock on You know card stock that looks like if you've ever seen a four-part form you remember those carbon copies You know like the golden rod the blue the like those are the colors we use Because they're ugly you want this you don't want this beautiful looking presentation You want something that looks ugly as hell so when they're mail they're going through their mail They're like what is this thing? You know, it's like the ugly sales letter same thing. It's it's got to stand up Yeah, both styles. Yeah The only time I would ever use a photo or some sort of a color postcard is when you've got a photo That is so powerful that there's no way you can replace with words with the picture says and those are rare They do they do come about you know, I have used ones very six one very successfully If you're in the weight loss industry and you've got a before and after picture That's really dramatic that you can tell it's dramatic even on a small photo. I would use it If you've you know something like that, but it's got to be pretty dramatic something you couldn't do with words The only one I've ever gotten to work better than just a plain text postcard was a picture of me sitting at my desk with like $50,000 worth of checks and money orders and I was selling a mail order course. So, you know, it was obviously like whoa It Russell was talking earlier about like people cutting out the coupon and can you imagine them sending them a you know We've got one campaign right now that people literally mail us checks and money orders about $2,000 a day Like still in what year is this? It's crazy Yes I don't think there's a way around it personally I think that when we talked to her like last night I think that you need to start with a sales letter to them and Because your markets finite I wouldn't worry about it. I mean the letters the way to go You'll go to your second Okay Right No, no, no, no wouldn't try to do it. Yeah, I wouldn't try to do it all in one I would take your highest possible Profit item first, you know the vacation homes probably use that to build buyers and responders list and the responders list from that now You own and you can mail your other stuff as the test part You know now you don't have to rent lists for that. You know, it's like having your house file Well to determine whether I want to You know put any effort into a new list that I've just discovered That's the thing that I'm gonna look at but if you're asking will I mail back farther? I'll absolutely mail back further There's lists that I found that So let's do I'll tell you one more thing about lists When you go to test one you want to use you want to ask the list broker for You want it to be nth And so what that means is that you're they're going to take a list that maybe has you know a hundred thousand names on it and you don't First if you want if you're looking for your best result you want to test the hotline So like let's say that you want to test you're only willing to commit to doing a thousand piece test You want to commit you want their most recent? Hotline list, okay, except for in very few cases But once you've tested that before you go buck wild and decide that you're gonna go like mail the whole list You want to do an nth test where you come back to them? And you say okay, so let's say that this first thousand on the hotline got you know phenomenal results And you said wow rather than let and this happens a lot Okay, the hotline is only you know five thousand names a month So you're not gonna get rich off that because even if it did well there's only five thousand names to mail So in those cases a lot of times I'm seeing dollar signs and I go yeah, but the universe is a hundred thousand names So what you want to do is you want to go back to them and say okay? I want to do a second test and this time I want nth names So what they'll do is they'll take one name from it like if it's a thousand and there's a hundred thousand names They'll take a hundred names from each thousand going back all the way so that you're getting an even spread of age of them If that test works Then you can jump up and down sing and dance and you know you can mail the whole list and you have a pretty good idea Reasonably of what it's going to do unless you mail on a postal strike holiday or something crazy like that So there's a couple of things I did write a short little list of like why why direct mail sucks Because there there are some negatives Probably should have said these earlier Okay, the biggest reason that people don't do direct mail today is because of the assumed cost like wow You know $450 to reach a thousand people and you know, what's my response going to be and all that kind of stuff? In direct response all I care about is my cost per sale So you know whatever the ads cost you know if it cost you know $10,000 to mail a thousand but I knew that my cost per sale is only 30 bucks because 300 of those people are going to buy it Who cares so that's one thing that a lot of people say is like a barrier. I wouldn't I Wouldn't let that get in your way once you know your cost of sale you can just dial it in Some categories I keep using the word categories because I just I hate the word niche because nobody pronounces it right It's actually niche So when I say category I'm saying I'm meaning niche but in some industry some verticals The lists are so finite that direct mail doesn't make a lot of sense You know the available lists aren't you know if you have a mass market product or a product that is got a pretty decent size list I mean decent size like audience Which you can do a lot of research online to find out you can do your normal You know how many people are searching on this term kind of thing to get an idea of the interest level and then you can Dive into next mark and look at lists that are available and then the third thing that Sucks about direct mail really does suck. I don't like this part and this is the part that frustrates me to this day And that is there's lag time. It's not like you can turn on an ad and know in two hours what it's doing for you You've got to commit the time you've got to you know put it in there and then literally When I do a mailing on a new New product a new offer it takes me about two and a half weeks to get all the data that I'm going to get and so You know that parts of bummer the nice thing about having those barriers the the perceived cost and that lag time is There's very very little competition in most categories and so I can be a Half-assed marketer and make really really great money in direct mail today You couldn't in the 80s and 90s because all the players were there now all the players are online and it's like you Have the ocean to yourself How many times you get offers in your mailbox versus in your inbox today? You know that whole thing has shifted You know from it used to be people are excited to get something in their inbox. I know that was a long time ago, but People were right. Oh, I got an email Yeah, well, it's like that now with your in but nobody writes anything or sends anything physically in the mail So when you are you stand out automatically and when you're going for something That's a high ticket thing this postcard as a way to invite them to a webinar or tell a seminar I believe well, I don't believe I've done it. It's huge. It's great because you stand out. You're not the email You're not the little you know ad So that's the reasons that direct mail is horrible is the reason I like it keeps a lot of people out You have like For me yes, but forever category and and again this really really Depends on the offer and the price point and all of that kind of stuff Most of the products that I sell outside of webinars are going to be in the 39 49 59 $69 price range so my response has got to be high to make those profitable because they're profitable on the front end Okay, I'll give you some numbers So a good response rate to a telephone like if you're driving them to a telephone message I've had postcards that have gotten 22% Cold list Yeah If it doesn't do about eight or nine I pretty much move on You know, I would consider that's not what and I'm not talking about buyers. I'm talking about responders responded to the to the message You know, then we've got to filter them through our sales process those are the 22% though, I'm you know, I'm being outrageous that doesn't happen all the time I've got a current campaign right now. That's doing about 14 and a half real regular on a bunch of lists If you're driving them straight and that's driving to a phone call driving them to a URL The best I've ever done is 11% and most of the time it's somewhere around Seven and a half to nine and a half or so so between seventy five and ninety five opt-ins per thousand and when I tell people that they go well Shit $450 per thousand Wow, so you're paying like seven or eight bucks per opt-in. Yeah Yeah, which is a lot higher than you'd pay online. It's also a lot more valuable opt-in They went through a lot of hoops to get on your list Guess what I have opt-ins that opted in that way from a postcard that have not gotten off my list after being hammered for seven years You know, they will stick they stay they're invested They also show up to webinars and tele seminars at a much higher rate than people that register online Oh, yeah I don't know that exact number I should go back and look at that A lot Yes, the lifetime value of that customer is about three times higher For me So I you know I can afford to spend seven eight bucks Especially because most of the time we're liquidating that on the front there There's enough people buying something on the front that that that cost is gone anyway Yes, that's your small amount Yep Sometimes but a lot of lists don't lend themselves to the ability to just pull a little piece out there You know if I'm if I'm mailing to everybody that bought, you know, I Don't think Russell puts up his list, but let's say that Russell put up his list And I was mailing to everybody that bought one single one of his products. What's Russell's latest product that he? Okay, so imagine I just want to go rent the list of just the people who who bought that right the Russell doesn't put it up But lots of other owners do do that if I was doing that and there's only you know eight nine ten thousand names all Across the country there might not be enough in one geographic area to just pull them out if it's a big source Then yeah, I'm gonna pick the one that's No, I don't I Was gonna say you could pick the one that's closest to you But the problem with that is every state, you know, there's different different things going on You've got weather and issues that happen that's different when you're doing direct mail like a lot of times We'll do we'll be doing a mailing that has been going on for six months. You know, it's just the same mail going out every single week and then You know all of a sudden the numbers are just way off and I'm like what the hell you know Oh, yeah, well, there's tornadoes across the Midwest and it just took everybody out. Nobody's getting their mail You know so can happen So you got to keep your eye on that stuff Nothing Yeah, no follow-up on on that list unless I've gotten a really fantastic response We talked about this earlier. I would rent the list again and mail it But the follow-up is going to be the same the follow-up after they've responded is going to be the same as it would be for online I mean, you know, you're going to walk them right through the same exact sequence. This is just another lead funnel All right Hopefully that was it. I know it's kind of a dull subject, but hopefully it you know, you got something out of it I mean, it's really the only exciting thing about it for most people that are already doing marketing is the idea that you can Go in and just pick your list that part's exciting and when you go on next mark and Go in there and start fooling around with it I've talked to lots people that said, you know, I spent six hours on there yesterday looking at all the lists that are available for my category So have some fun with that one more question I used to teach it I I'm one of I I've got nothing to sell This is a favor to Russell I like Russell Yeah, so Currently no, but if you just you know, my name is Luke Jaten J A T E N In two or three months, we're probably going to have a revised version I've got a course that we've been working on and but I I hate to commit to a time because I've said that it was coming out for a long time and The problem is I'm busy doing this stuff and so the teaching it is fun and it makes money But I make a lot more money doing it But I've got a course coming out supposedly the summer called modern mail order where I'm actually going to go through much more than just the postcard stuff because You know, this is what Russell wanted me to talk about today But we do SMS and we do, you know mobile and we do and I've done lots of my campaigns have been Magazine ads and radio and TV like you name a lead source. I've used it And so that's one of the things that I was trying to get you to get with this is don't get hung up on or like Think that you know one lead source is a little shiny object You got to go chase because they most of especially the online ones and most of them don't have a big long shelf life You know you get in there. They work whatever the sales funnel the black box Russell's talking about that's much more permanent The lead flow get it from wherever you can learn a lot of different lead sources. The more you have the better So if you If you friend me on Facebook, I'll keep you posted that's probably the easiest way to do it That's it big finish
